name: autoskills
description: One command. Your entire AI skill stack. Installed.
readme_summary: Autoskills is a command-line tool that automatically detects a project's technology stack and installs relevant AI agent skills from a curated registry. It simplifies the process of integrating AI capabilities into development workflows by handling the detection, selection, and installation of necessary skill files.
key_features: Support for a wide range of technologies
primary_use_case: To automate the installation of AI agent skills based on a project's detected technology stack, streamlining the integration of AI into development.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly robot architect standing in a bustling construction site, holding a blueprint that magically transforms into a vibrant, interconnected network of glowing AI agent icons. The robot is scanning the site, which is filled with subtle visual cues of different technologies like code snippets, UI elements, and server racks. Small, glowing data streams flow from the blueprint to the relevant areas of the construction site, representing the automated installation of AI skills. The scene is bright and optimistic, with a clean, 3D isometric illustration style and a color palette of blues, greens, and oranges, emphasizing efficiency and innovation.

name: runtipi
description: Runtipi is a homeserver for everyone! One command setup, one click installs for your favorites self-hosted apps. ✨
readme_summary: Runtipi is a user-friendly homeserver orchestrator built with TypeScript, NestJS, and React. It simplifies the management and deployment of self-hosted applications using Docker, accessible through a web interface and an app store.
key_features: Extensible app store with community options
primary_use_case: To provide an easy-to-use platform for individuals to set up and manage their own personal homeserver with various self-hosted applications.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized robot gardener tending to a vibrant, miniature cityscape of diverse self-hosted applications (like a small cloud server, a media player icon, a smart home hub). The robot is using a watering can that sprinkles digital data streams onto the apps, making them grow and connect. Small, clean UI elements showing app icons and status indicators are subtly integrated into the scene. The overall mood is welcoming and efficient, with a clean, bright aesthetic. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with soft lighting and clear, friendly character design, emphasizing ease of use and centralized control.

name: PolyCut
description: Polycut is a tool designed to import SVG files and convert them to 2D GCode to run on 3D Printers that have blades/pens/knives/foil stampers or other tools attached. It also directly supports uploading to a networked 3D Printer via Klipper. Think of it as an alternative to buying a Cricut if you already have a 3D Printer.
readme_summary: Polycut is a .NET application that converts SVG files into 2D GCode, enabling 3D printers and CNC machines equipped with cutting or drawing tools to execute designs. It offers features for importing, manipulating, and previewing vector graphics, and can directly upload GCode to Klipper-enabled printers.
key_features: Import and manipulate SVG files (group/layer preservation, boolean operations, transformations)
Support for various tool modes: Cutting, Drawing (with multiple fill patterns), Multipass, Foiling/Engraving
Configurable tool path optimization for drag knives
Save and reload projects, export canvas to SVG
Manage multiple printer profiles with custom GCode and offsets
Klipper bounding box preview for alignment confirmation
2D animated toolpath preview with pause/resume/step controls
Direct upload to networked 3D printers via Moonraker/Klipper
primary_use_case: To enable users to repurpose their 3D printers as vinyl cutters, plotters, or engraving machines by converting SVG designs into GCode.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled artisan, akin to a modern-day scribe, is meticulously transforming intricate SVG blueprints into tangible, flowing lines on a material. The artisan holds a digital stylus, and the blueprints materialize from a glowing tablet, flowing like liquid metal into a precise path traced by a robotic arm holding a fine engraving tool. The robotic arm is mounted on a 3D printer bed, suggesting the repurposing of existing hardware. Subtle digital particles and faint GCode snippets float around the scene, hinting at the underlying software. The overall mood is one of creative transformation and precision engineering, rendered in a clean, 3D isometric illustration style with a vibrant, yet focused color palette.

name: arc-kit
description: The Enterprise Architecture Governance Harness — strategy, architecture, delivery, and assurance for AI coding assistants
readme_summary: ArcKit is an enterprise architecture governance toolkit that streamlines strategy, design, delivery, and assurance workflows. It leverages AI assistance to manage architecture principles, stakeholder drivers, risk, business cases, requirements, data modeling, technology research, strategic planning, diagram generation, vendor management, design reviews, and traceability.
key_features: Analyzing stakeholder drivers, goals, and outcomes
Business case justification (HM Treasury Green Book SOBC)
Data modeling with ERD, GDPR compliance, and data governance
Technology research with build vs buy analysis (web search powered)
Azure-specific research using Microsoft Learn MCP
Managing vendor RFP and selection processes
primary_use_case: To provide a structured, AI-assisted workflow for enterprise architects to manage and govern all aspects of their architecture, from strategy to delivery and assurance.
cover_image_prompt: A master architect, depicted as a wise conductor, standing at a grand podium overlooking a sprawling, complex city. The conductor's baton, glowing with digital energy, orchestrates various elements: blueprints unfurling into buildings, data streams flowing into organized structures, and stakeholder goals appearing as illuminated beacons. Small screens embedded in the podium display code snippets and governance metrics. The city itself is a blend of physical structures and digital networks, representing enterprise architecture. The scene is bathed in a warm, authoritative light, emphasizing control and foresight. The style is a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, organized aesthetic, clearly showing the transformation of abstract concepts into a functional, governed enterprise.

name: thunderbolt
description: AI You Control: Choose your models. Own your data. Eliminate vendor lock-in.
readme_summary: Thunderbolt is an open-source, cross-platform AI client designed for on-premise deployment, allowing users to choose their AI models, own their data, and avoid vendor lock-in. It supports a wide range of platforms and AI models, with features geared towards enterprise use.
key_features: Available on web, iOS, Android, Mac, Linux, Windows
primary_use_case: To provide a flexible and controllable AI client solution for enterprises and individuals who want to deploy AI models on their own infrastructure, manage their data, and avoid reliance on third-party services.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled artisan in a workshop, meticulously assembling a complex, customizable automaton from various interchangeable parts representing different AI models (e.g., a brain, a circuit board, a data stream). The artisan is shown selecting a specific 'gear' (model) and fitting it into the automaton, which then glows with intelligence. In the background, a secure vault symbolizes data ownership, and a clear 'no vendor lock' sign is visible. The scene is brightly lit with a clean, modern aesthetic, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with subtle digital particle effects emanating from the active automaton.

name: DeepGEMM
description: DeepGEMM: clean and efficient FP8 GEMM kernels with fine-grained scaling
readme_summary: DeepGEMM is a high-performance CUDA kernel library for modern large language models, offering optimized GEMM operations for various precisions (FP8, FP4, BF16) and advanced features like fused MoE and MQA scoring. It utilizes a Just-In-Time (JIT) compilation approach for flexibility and performance without requiring CUDA compilation during installation.
key_features: Optimized GEMM kernels for FP8, FP4, and BF16 precisions
Fused MoE with overlapped communication (Mega MoE)
Support for NVIDIA SM90 and SM100 architectures
Clean and accessible CUDA codebase for kernel optimization learning
primary_use_case: Providing highly optimized matrix multiplication (GEMM) kernels for accelerating computations in large language models and other deep learning applications on NVIDIA GPUs.
cover_image_prompt: A master artisan in a futuristic forge, meticulously crafting intricate gears and circuits from raw, glowing energy. The artisan is shown using specialized tools to shape streams of FP8 and FP4 data (represented as shimmering, colored particles) into perfectly interlocking components that form a complex, powerful engine. The engine hums with potential, symbolizing the accelerated computations of large language models. Subtle holographic blueprints and glowing code snippets float around the forge, hinting at the underlying software. The scene is set in a clean, modern workshop with dramatic, focused lighting. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a vibrant, technological color palette.
